(born 1 July 1987) is an English [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") and [wrestler](/wiki/Wrestler "Wrestler") who competes in the [welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight_(MMA) "Welterweight (MMA)") division. He is one of the most accomplished modern-day [catch wrestlers](/wiki/Catch_wrestler "Catch wrestler"), having won three [Snake Pit World Championships](/wiki/Snake_Pit_World_Championships "Snake Pit World Championships") and a [Snake Pit British Championship](/wiki/Snake_Pit_British_Championships "Snake Pit British Championships").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English mixed martial arts fighter

This article is about the martial artist. For the Australian politician, see [John Hathaway (Australian politician)](/wiki/John_Hathaway_(Australian_politician) "John Hathaway (Australian politician)"). For the Florida politician, see [John M. Hathaway](/wiki/John_M._Hathaway "John M. Hathaway").

**John Lawrence Hathaway** (born 1 July 1987) is an English [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") and [wrestler](/wiki/Wrestler "Wrestler") who competes in the [welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight_(MMA) "Welterweight (MMA)") division. He is one of the most accomplished modern-day [catch wrestlers](/wiki/Catch_wrestler "Catch wrestler"), having won three [Snake Pit World Championships](/wiki/Snake_Pit_World_Championships "Snake Pit World Championships") and a [Snake Pit British Championship](/wiki/Snake_Pit_British_Championships "Snake Pit British Championships").

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Hathaway&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Background and early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Hathaway&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Background and early career")]

Formerly an open-side flanker for local rugby team [Hove RFC](/wiki/Hove_Rugby_Club "Hove Rugby Club"), Hathaway decided to make the transition to mixed martial arts after watching the Ultimate Fighting championship on television. He made his professional MMA debut on 25 June 2006, defeating his opponent via rear naked choke in the first round.

John Hathaway currently trains at London Shootfighters.[[7]](#cite_note-mirror.co.uk-7) Hathaway also trained in the US with [American Top Team](/wiki/American_Top_Team "American Top Team") and [10th Planet Jiu Jitsu](/wiki/10th_Planet_Jiu_Jitsu "10th Planet Jiu Jitsu").[[7]](#cite_note-mirror.co.uk-7)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Hathaway&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

After signing a four fight deal, Hathaway was scheduled to fight undefeated Tom Egan at [UFC 93](/wiki/UFC_93 "UFC 93"). He made an impressive UFC debut, scoring a dominant first round TKO win over Egan via elbows.

Hathaway defeated the debuting [Rick Story](/wiki/Rick_Story "Rick Story") via unanimous decision at [UFC 99](/wiki/UFC_99 "UFC 99").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Hathaway's next fight was against [Paul Taylor](/wiki/Paul_Taylor_(fighter) "Paul Taylor (fighter)") at [UFC 105](/wiki/UFC_105 "UFC 105").[[9]](#cite_note-9) Hathaway won a unanimous decision (30-27, 30-27, 30-26).

Hathaway then faced the biggest fight of his career as he fought [Diego Sanchez](/wiki/Diego_Sanchez "Diego Sanchez") on 29 May 2010 at [UFC 114](/wiki/UFC_114 "UFC 114"), in Sanchez' return to welterweight.[[10]](#cite_note-10) In the first round, Hathaway dropped Sanchez with a knee to the head, as Sanchez attempted a takedown. Hathaway then dominated with ground and pound. The rest of the fight saw Hathaway utilize his reach advantage to dominate the striking, which gave him the unanimous decision victory.

Hathaway was expected to face [Dong Hyun Kim](/wiki/Dong_Hyun_Kim "Dong Hyun Kim") at [UFC 120](/wiki/UFC_120 "UFC 120"),[[11]](#cite_note-11) but Kim was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Mike Pyle](/wiki/Mike_Pyle_(fighter) "Mike Pyle (fighter)").[[12]](#cite_note-12) Hathaway lost to Pyle via unanimous decision after being overmatched by the heavy underdog. This loss was also the first of his career.

Hathaway fought [Kris McCray](/wiki/Kris_McCray "Kris McCray") on 26 March 2011 at [UFC Fight Night 24](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_24 "UFC Fight Night 24").[[13]](#cite_note-13) The fight was closely contested throughout. However, Hathaway walked away the winner via split decision.

Hathaway was expected to face [Pascal Krauss](/wiki/Pascal_Krauss "Pascal Krauss") on 5 November 2011 at [UFC 138](/wiki/UFC_138 "UFC 138").[[14]](#cite_note-14) However, on 30 August Krauss pulled out of the bout citing a shoulder injury, and was replaced by [Matt Brown](/wiki/Matt_Brown_(fighter) "Matt Brown (fighter)").[[15]](#cite_note-BrownIn-15) On 17 October, Hathaway himself was forced to pull out of the bout due to an undisclosed injury. As a result, Brown was pulled from the card and shifted to [UFC 139](/wiki/UFC_139 "UFC 139").[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Hathaway/Krauss took place on 5 May 2012 at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3").[[17]](#cite_note-17) He won the fight via unanimous decision.

Hathaway beat [John Maguire](/wiki/John_Maguire_(fighter) "John Maguire (fighter)") via unanimous decision on 29 September 2012 at [UFC on Fuel TV 5](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV_5 "UFC on Fuel TV 5").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Hathaway was expected to face [Erick Silva](/wiki/Erick_Silva "Erick Silva") on 8 June 2013 at [UFC on Fuel TV 10](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV:_Nogueira_vs._Werdum "UFC on Fuel TV: Nogueira vs. Werdum").[[19]](#cite_note-19) However, Hathaway was pulled from the bout in late April and replaced by [Jason High](/wiki/Jason_High "Jason High").[[20]](#cite_note-HathawayOut-20)

Hathaway returned from his extended hiatus to face [Dong Hyun Kim](/wiki/Dong_Hyun_Kim "Dong Hyun Kim") on 1 March 2014 at [The Ultimate Fighter: China Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_China#UFC_Fight_Night:_Kim_vs._Hathaway "The Ultimate Fighter: China").[[21]](#cite_note-21) He lost the fight via third round knockout due to a spinning back elbow.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

Hathaway was expected to face [Gunnar Nelson](/wiki/Gunnar_Nelson_(fighter) "Gunnar Nelson (fighter)") on 11 July 2015 at [UFC 189](/wiki/UFC_189 "UFC 189").[[23]](#cite_note-23) However, Hathaway pulled out of the bout on 23 June citing injury, and was replaced by [Brandon Thatch](/wiki/Brandon_Thatch "Brandon Thatch").[[24]](#cite_note-24)

### Return from retirement

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Hathaway&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Return from retirement")]

After almost 8 years after his last bout, Hathaway made his return on October 15, 2022, at Oktagon 36 against André Ricardo.[[25]](#cite_note-25) He won his return, dominating the bout for a unanimous decision victory.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

Hathaway faced Łukasz Siwiec on June 17, 2023 at Oktagon 44, losing the bout via unanimous decision.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

## Catch wrestling career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Hathaway&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Catch wrestling career")]

During his MMA career, Hathaway became known for his wrestling ability.[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30) During his time away from MMA, he began competing in [catch wrestling](/wiki/Catch_wrestling "Catch wrestling"). In November 2018, Hathaway competed at [Catch Wrestling World Championships](/wiki/Catch_Wrestling_World_Championships "Catch Wrestling World Championships") hosted by [The Snake Pit](/wiki/The_Snake_Pit_(Wigan) "The Snake Pit (Wigan)") at the [University of Bolton Stadium](/wiki/University_of_Bolton_Stadium "University of Bolton Stadium"), winning the light heavyweight (90 kg) championship.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[31]](#cite_note-:0-31)

He returned for the [2019 World Championships](/wiki/2019_Catch_Wrestling_World_Championships "2019 Catch Wrestling World Championships"), winning another championship, this time at middleweight (82 kg). Also at the event, Hathaway competed in a challenge match against Nathaniel Brown, a British medallist in freestyle wrestling, which Hathaway won. He received the Ian Bromley trophy as "best male wrestler of the night."[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3)

Due to postponements because of the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic"), the Snake Pit's next Catch Wrestling World Championships were held in [June 2022](/wiki/2022_Catch_Wrestling_World_Championships "2022 Catch Wrestling World Championships"). Hathaway competed again, winning the middleweight (82 kg) championship and receiving the Jack Carroll award for fastest win (by pin or submission) at the event.[[4]](#cite_note-:3-4)

Hathaway competed at the [Snake Pit British Championships 2024](/wiki/Snake_Pit_British_Championships_2024 "Snake Pit British Championships 2024"), winning the light heavyweight (90 kg) championship,[[6]](#cite_note-:5-6) followed by the [Snake Pit World Championships](/wiki/Snake_Pit_World_Championships_2024 "Snake Pit World Championships 2024"), where he was the middleweight (82 kg) runner-up.[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[32]](#cite_note-:6-32)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Hathaway&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Hathaway was diagnosed with [Crohn's disease](/wiki/Crohn%27s_disease "Crohn's disease") in 2010.[[33]](#cite_note-33) This prevented him from fighting from 2014 to 2022.

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Hathaway&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

### Catch wrestling

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Hathaway&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Catch wrestling")]

* **[The Snake Pit](/wiki/The_Snake_Pit_(Wigan) "The Snake Pit (Wigan)")**
  + [Catch Wrestling World Championships](/wiki/Catch_Wrestling_World_Championships "Catch Wrestling World Championships")
    - [2018 - Champion, light heavyweight](/wiki/2018_Catch_Wrestling_World_Championships "2018 Catch Wrestling World Championships") (90 kg)[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[31]](#cite_note-:0-31)
    - [2019 - Champion, middleweight](/wiki/2019_Catch_Wrestling_World_Championships "2019 Catch Wrestling World Championships") (82 kg)[[3]](#cite_note-:2-3)
      * Ian Bromley trophy as "best male wrestler of the night"
    - [2022 - Champion, middleweight](/wiki/2022_Catch_Wrestling_World_Championships "2022 Catch Wrestling World Championships") (82 kg)[[4]](#cite_note-:3-4)
      * Jack Carroll award for fastest win (by pin or submission)
    - [2024 - Runner-up, middleweight](/wiki/2024_Catch_Wrestling_World_Championships "2024 Catch Wrestling World Championships") (82 kg)[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[32]](#cite_note-:6-32)
  + [Catch Wrestling British Championships](/wiki/Catch_Wrestling_British_Championships "Catch Wrestling British Championships")
    - [2024 - Champion, light heavyweight](/wiki/2024_Catch_Wrestling_British_Championships "2024 Catch Wrestling British Championships") (90 kg)[[6]](#cite_note-:5-6)

### Mixed martial arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Hathaway&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts")]

* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + **UFC.com Awards**
    - 2009: [Ranked #9 Newcomer of the Year](/wiki/2009_in_UFC#2009_UFC.com_awards "2009 in UFC")[[34]](#cite_note-34)
    - 2010: [Ranked #3 Upset of the Year](/wiki/2010_in_UFC#2010_UFC.com_awards "2010 in UFC") vs. [Diego Sanchez](/wiki/Diego_Sanchez "Diego Sanchez")[[35]](#cite_note-35)
